segments.title: Technology, destroyers, and historical emphasis
segments.summary: The Patreon live frames Clarke's disagreements with other historians around emphasis, then starts with technology determinism and the undercounted value of destroyers.
segments.body: The discussion captures the live stream's organizing premise and its first substantive arc. Clarke contrasts his own five areas of disagreement with an AI-generated list, argues that technology rarely works as a single guaranteed war-winning factor, and then applies the same critique to histories that reduce destroyers to anonymous counts rather than treating them as varied, tactically important warships.

evidence.note: The opening explains the five-disagreement format and says many historian differences are matters of emphasis and perspective before starting with technology.
The technology section rejects silver-bullet explanations and stresses doctrine, training, tactics, and the ability to use a technology.
The destroyer section argues that destroyer types, roles, leadership effects, and overlooked actions matter rather than treating them as generic counts.
